title: Mammillaria greggii
taxonomy: Mammillaria greggii is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is a type of mammillaria, a diverse and widespread genus of small to medium-sized cacti. Within the genus, Mammillaria greggii is classified as part of the subgenus Mammillaria, which includes species with small, rounded stems and short spines. The species is further distinguished by its short, stout stems, fine white spines, and funnel-shaped flowers. Mammillaria greggii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.

intro: Mammillaria greggii, also known as Gregg's nipple cactus or mammillaria, is a species of flowering cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Mammillaria greggii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mammillaria greggii is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
